name: Llama 3
summary: Meta's open flagship large language model.
description: Llama 3 is Meta's third-generation open LLM. It is released in 8B and 70B parameter sizes with a 128k vocabulary tokenizer, a 128k context window, and improved reasoning and instruction-following performance over Llama 2.

timeline.title: Llama 3 released
timeline.description: Open weights for 8B and 70B parameter chat and base models.

timeline.title: Llama 3.1 405B
timeline.description: Frontier-class open weights released under a permissive license.

name: Mistral 7B
description: Mistral 7B demonstrated that a carefully trained 7B model could outperform the larger Llama 2 13B across reasoning, math, and code benchmarks. It popularized sliding-window attention and grouped-query attention in the open ecosystem.

timeline.title: Mistral 7B released
timeline.description: First Mistral open release, Apache 2.0 licensed.

timeline.title: Mistral 7B v0.2
timeline.description: Extended vocabulary and improved instruction following.

name: Stable Diffusion XL
summary: Two-stage text-to-image model with stunning fidelity.
description: Stable Diffusion XL (SDXL) combines a base UNet and a refiner UNet trained at 1024×1024 resolution. It dramatically improves photorealism, prompt adherence, and typography over the SD 1.5 baseline.

timeline.title: SDXL 1.0 released
timeline.description: Base + refiner pipeline, open weights under RAIL-M.

name: Qwen2.5
summary: Alibaba's broad family of strong, permissively licensed open LLMs.
description: Qwen2.5 is a family of open language models from Alibaba spanning 0.5B to 72B parameters, with specialised Coder and Math variants. It offers long context, strong multilingual and coding performance, and is one of the most widely used open-weight families on Hugging Face.

timeline.title: Qwen2.5 released
timeline.description: Base, instruct, coder, and math variants from 0.5B to 72B.

name: Gemma 2
summary: Google's lightweight open models built from Gemini research.
description: Gemma 2 is Google's family of open-weight text models in 2B, 9B, and 27B sizes, derived from the research behind Gemini. It delivers strong performance for its size and is designed to run efficiently on a single GPU or laptop.

name: DeepSeek-R1
summary: Open reasoning model rivaling closed frontier models on math and code.
description: DeepSeek-R1 is an open-weight reasoning model trained with large-scale reinforcement learning to produce long chain-of-thought before answering. It reaches performance competitive with leading closed reasoning models on math, code, and logic, and ships distilled smaller variants that run locally.

timeline.title: DeepSeek-R1 released
timeline.description: Open reasoning model plus distilled 1.5B–70B variants under MIT.

name: Mixtral 8x7B
summary: Sparse mixture-of-experts model with the quality of a much larger LLM.
description: Mixtral 8x7B is a sparse mixture-of-experts (MoE) model that routes each token to 2 of 8 expert networks, giving the quality of a large model at the inference cost of a ~13B model. It popularised open MoE and remains a strong, efficient Apache-licensed option.

timeline.title: Mixtral 8x7B released
timeline.description: First widely used open sparse MoE, Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Whisper
summary: OpenAI's open speech-to-text model for transcription and translation.
description: Whisper is an open-source automatic speech recognition model trained on 680k hours of multilingual audio. It transcribes and translates speech robustly across languages and accents, and its open weights power countless local transcription tools and the faster-whisper runtime.

timeline.title: Whisper released
timeline.description: Open multilingual ASR under MIT; large-v3 followed in 2023.

name: FLUX.1
summary: State-of-the-art open text-to-image model from the original SD team.
description: FLUX.1 is a 12B-parameter rectified-flow transformer for text-to-image generation, created by Black Forest Labs (founders of the original Stable Diffusion). The [schnell] variant is Apache-2.0 and runs locally, producing high-fidelity images with excellent prompt adherence and typography.

name: Phi-3
summary: Microsoft's small models that punch far above their size.
description: Phi-3 is a family of small open language models from Microsoft trained on carefully curated, 'textbook-quality' data. The 3.8B Mini model rivals much larger models on reasoning benchmarks and is small enough to run comfortably on a phone or laptop.

timeline.title: Phi-3 released
timeline.description: Mini (3.8B), Small (7B), and Medium (14B) under MIT.
